pattern: Counting how often each character appears and pairing every letter or digit with its position-reversed counterpart lets a single pass over the 36 possible characters accumulate the total absolute frequency gap, taking care to only count each mirrored pair once.
hints: First tally how many times every lowercase letter and every digit appears anywhere in the string.
For a letter, its mirror is the letter the same distance from 'z' as it is from 'a'; for a digit, its mirror is the digit the same distance from '9' as it is from '0'.

Walk through the alphabet and digits once, and only add abs(freq(c) - freq(mirror(c))) the first time you encounter each mirrored pair -- for example only process it when c comes before its mirror, so the pair isn't counted twice.

edge_inputs.description: a mix of letters and digits with several mirror pairs
a pair whose frequencies already match contributes nothing
a single character has no mirror partner present
the mirrored pair must be counted only once even though both of its characters appear in the string
digits and letters must not be mixed together as mirror partners
every letter appears with its exact mirror in equal amounts
digits filling both ends of the mirrored digit range

stress.description: random long string of lowercase letters and digits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    alphabet = string.ascii_lowercase + '0123456789'
    n = max(1, min(size, 500000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
